zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Beauchamp於大約1278年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特哈契時，她的父親John de Beauchamp11276歲，母親Cecily de Vivonne22歲。她於大約1292年與Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1341年11月18日在英國格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor de Beauchamp于大约1278年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特哈契时，她的父亲John de Beauchamp11276岁，母亲Cecily de Vivonne22岁。她于大约1292年与Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1341年11月18日在英国格盧斯特斯爾恩波利享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Beauchamp was born about 1278, in Hatch, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, John de Beauchamp, was 11276 and her mother, Cecily de Vivonne, was 22. She married Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin about 1292. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 18 November 1341, in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Beauchamp est née environ 1278, à Hatch, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Beauchamp, avait 11276 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Vivonne, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin environ 1292. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 18 novembre 1341, à Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor de Beauchamp um 1278 in Hatch, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Beauchamp 11276 und ihre Mutter Cecily de Vivonne 22. Sie hat um 1292 Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 18. November 1341 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Beauchamp è nata il circa 1278 nel Hatch, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John de Beauchamp, aveva 11276 anni e sua madre, Cecily de Vivonne, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin circa 1292. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 18 novembre 1341, nel Thornbury, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Beauchampが約1278年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトハトチで生まれた時，父John de Beauchampは11276歳，母Cecily de Vivonneは22歳であった。約1292年にFulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1341年11月18日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアソルンブリーで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1278년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 해트치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Beauchamp(은)는 11276세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Vivonne(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1292년 Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1341년 11월 18일 영국 그루스스터실르 토른버이에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1278, em Hatch, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Beauchamp, tinha 11276 anos e sua mãe, Cecily de Vivonne, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin aproximadamente 1292. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 18 de novembro de 1341, em Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Beauchamp родилась около 1278 г. в местности хач, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Beauchamp было 11276 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Vivonne, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin около 1292 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 18 ноября 1341 г. в местности таурнбури, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1278, en Hatch, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Beauchamp, tenía 11276 años y su mamá, Cecily de Vivonne, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Fulk FitzWarin 2nd Baron FitzWarin aproximadamente 1292. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 18 de noviembre de 1341, en Thornbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años.
